svg.text: DCP Protocol Stack — Five Layers
abstract-model-first, transport-last · D2/D4/D5 add the Registry & Hosting layer (§3)
1 · Dialogue State Layer
The authoritative state of a running instance — what the dialogue is and where it stands:
instance_id · template_ref · goal · termination_policy · status · turn · messages[] · events[] · open_gates[] · pending_inputs[] · budget
fully replayable from the append-only log 〔D3〕
2 · Participation Layer
Who participates and how: registered participants 〔D4〕 · role casting · access tiers & visibility 〔D5〕 ·
required / optional / gate humans · open mic · observers (observe tier) · joining & leaving
the key differentiator from agent-to-agent protocols: participants are not assumed to be autonomous agents
3 · Orchestration Layer
How the dialogue evolves: select_speaker · inject_context · request_human · request_revision · request_verification ·
resolve_gate · stop — with pre-action and post-action oversight and a per-turn termination check
makes the dialogue controllable rather than purely emergent
4 · Registry & Hosting Layer
NEW
The server-level surface 〔D2, D4, D5〕: one Registry, two catalogs — TemplateCatalog · ParticipantCatalog.
Operations: register_template · list/get_template · register_participant · list/get_participant · instantiate · join · leave · restore
Access & auth: admission, tier assignment, visibility, bearer-token authentication via pluggable Authenticator 〔D6〕
semantic operations — defined independently of transport (this is NOT the delivery layer)
5 · Delivery Layer
How records reach clients — implementation-specific bindings: HTTP API · SSE · WebSocket · polling · batch replay ·
frontend rendering · token-level streaming
the semantic protocol MUST NOT depend on any particular delivery mechanism (SSE streaming is a valid choice, not a requirement)
semantic protocol
bindings
